section.heading: Statute of limitations
section.chapeau: Any action to enforce any liability under this subchapter may be brought before the later of—

paragraph.content: the end of the 5-year period beginning on the date of the occurrence of the violation involved; or

paragraph.chapeau: in any case in which any credit repair organization has materially and willfully misrepresented any information which—

subparagraph.content: the credit repair organization is required, by any provision of this subchapter, to disclose to any consumer; and
is material to the establishment of the credit repair organization’s liability to the consumer under this subchapter,

paragraph.continuation: the end of the 5-year period beginning on the date of the discovery by the consumer of the misrepresentation.

section.sourceCredit: (Pub. L. 90–321, title IV, § 411, as added Pub. L. 104–208, div. A, title II, § 2451, Sept. 30, 1996, 110 Stat. 3009–461.)

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: Section applicable after the end of the 6-month period beginning on Sept. 30, 1996, except with respect to contracts entered into by a credit repair organization before the end of such period, see section 413 of Pub. L. 90–321, as added by Pub. L. 104–208, set out as a note under section 1679 of this title.
